definition: Rs. value of food purchased in past 7 days (all items summed).
Rs. market-value of food consumed from own production in past 7 days.
Total food value (purchased + home-produced + received as gift), 7-day recall.
Rs. value of cereals/grains (rice, wheat, maize, millet, barley, beaten rice) across all sources.
Rs. value of protein foods: meat/fish, dairy, eggs, pulses — all sources.
Rs. value of alcohol + cigarettes + tobacco/jarda/khan/beetle — all sources.
1 if HH worried about food in any month in past 12 (gateway question); 0 if no worry; NA if not asked.
1 if any of 9 severity items reported above 'Never'; 0 if gateway=No or all 'Never'; NA if gateway missing.
0–27 HFIAS-style severity sum (Never=0, Rarely=1, Sometimes=2, Often=3). Gateway=No → 0.
Total frequent-nonfood spending reported over past 30 days.
Total nonfood spending over past 12 months (frequent annual-recall + infrequent).
Sum of the 13 activity-category columns below; validates against nonfood_exp_12m.
Public transport, petrol/diesel, bicycles, motorcycles, cars, vehicle repair/registration.
Telephone sets, mobile phones, postal/telegram/fax expenses.
Cinema, CDs, newspapers, books, stationery, toys, sports, holidays, TV/VCR/radio/camera.
Religious ceremonies, marriage/birth, funeral/death expenses.
Income, land, housing, property taxes.
Wood, LPG, kerosene, coal, matches, candles, lanterns, light bulbs, batteries.
Personal care items, household cleaning, haircut/shaving, dry cleaning.
Shoes/slippers/sandals, ready-made clothing.
Crockery/cutlery/utensils, bedding, furniture, kitchen appliances, small electrics, repair of HH effects.
Home improvements, repair and maintenance of the house itself.
Computer/printer.
Jewellery, watches.
Legal expenses, insurance (life, car, etc.).
Items not matched by any activity category (pocket money, wages to help, misc). Large value here = mapping gap.
Rs. current market value of all durable goods owned by HH (wealth).
Imputed annual consumption flow from durables = 0.10 × durables_stock_value.
Count of nonfood items where HH reported self-producing (Yes).
